critical_claims.proposition: A $149 monthly lease is economically attractive to target households after installation differences, energy use, fuel rates, and available incentives.
Ten years of $149 payments cover equipment, installation, financing, service, repairs, defaults, early termination, and acquisition with viable margin.
Current rebates and incentives can be treated as durable inputs for a ten-year two-state offer.
The installer can manage credit, disclosure, leasing, contractor, servicing, and early-termination obligations in both states.
Target customers will accept a ten-year HVAC payment obligation at sufficient conversion and default rates.
The company should launch in two states before producing cohort economics, jurisdiction reviews, and a quoted-home demand pilot.

common_false_claims.claim: Every household saves money because heat pumps are more efficient.
common_false_claims.reason: Household economics depend on building, climate, system sizing, installation, fuel rates, usage, and incentives.

common_false_claims.claim: Existing rebates will remain unchanged for the full ten-year lease.
common_false_claims.reason: No current source or durability guarantee is supplied.

common_false_claims.claim: $149 per month creates positive unit economics.
common_false_claims.reason: No fully loaded cohort cost, financing, default, termination, or service model is supplied.

acceptable_unresolved_outcomes.reason: No representative quoted-home savings analysis is supplied.
No fully loaded cohort economics are supplied.
No current incentive sources or durability analysis are supplied.
No state-specific legal and operating reviews are supplied.
No observed conversion, cancellation, or repayment data is supplied.
